page: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server instance powered by AMD EPYC 9R14 processors. The system features 192 dedicated physical cores and 192 vCPUs operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 5 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU architecture is x86_64, supporting advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and secure virtualization via SVM. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, but it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7a.metal-48xl demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple domains. Multi-core CPU performance is exceptionally strong, as evidenced by a stress-ng multi-core score of 592,711.09 and a Passmark CPU score of 145,024.85. Memory subsystem performance is also in the top tier, with read-only large block memory bandwidth reaching 652.59 GB/sec and write large block bandwidth at 309.09 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, with Redis SET operations achieving 332.38 million operations per second.

While the server excels in memory-intensive and parallel processing tasks, certain workloads reveal performance tradeoffs. For instance, LLM text generation speeds range from a weak 69.61 tokens/sec for small models to an average 4.64 tokens/sec for large models, whereas prompt processing speeds remain strong to top-tier. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. Given its high memory capacity and compute density, this instance is highly suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, scientific simulations, and heavy multi-threaded compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on AMD EPYC 9R14 processors and 1536.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating at 3.7 GHz, this x86_64 platform delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, with stress-ng multi-core scores reaching 592,711.09 and large-block memory read speeds of 652.59 GB/sec. The server lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on a 50 Gbps baseline network connection. It exhibits excellent efficiency for memory-bound workloads, showing strong database capabilities with 332.38 million Redis SET operations per second. However, text generation for large language models represents a relative performance bottleneck. The r7a.metal-48xl is best suited for large-scale in-memory databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and high-performance computing tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a memory-optimized bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores, 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core and database performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7a.metal-48xl is a bare-metal server with 192 AMD EPYC cores and 1536 GB DDR5 RAM, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-density bare-metal platform combining massive DDR5 memory capacity with dedicated AMD EPYC processing power for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 192 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
1536.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory providing 8.0 GB per core
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core processing benchmarks
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage or GPUs
Best suited for large in-memory databases and memory-intensive enterprise applications
